text.text:
I’ve been wanting to try Out of Ordi for a while, so I lined up right at their 11:30 AM opening time on weekend and waited about 30 minutes to get inside. Inside were clean and modern.

I ended up trying 8 different flavors of their signature salt bread right away. Overall, the savory options stood out to me much more than the dessert/sweet ones. My absolute favorite was the Ordi Original—it was incredibly buttery and perfectly crispy.

Here is my personal ranking from most favorite to least favorite:
1 ORDI ORIGINAL
2 MENTA
3 SUPER MATCHA
4 TRIPLE TRUFFLE
5 CORN CREME
6 UBE AURA
7 YUZU-HAE
8 PISTACHIO BOMB

Eat them right away! While they are still good the next day, they aren't quite as soft as they are when fresh.

text.text: Small modern cute coffee & salt bread pick-up atmosphere with cute merch. A wide variety of delicious viral Korean salt bread. There are limit of 8 per customers and we tried almost all flavors. Very delicious and cute merch variety. Strongly recommend the viral Korean salt bread! There maybe a wait but line moves pretty fast and totally worth it!

originalText.text: Small modern cute coffee & salt bread pick-up atmosphere with cute merch. A wide variety of delicious viral Korean salt bread. There are limit of 8 per customers and we tried almost all flavors. Very delicious and cute merch variety. Strongly recommend the viral Korean salt bread! There maybe a wait but line moves pretty fast and totally worth it!

text.text:
OUT OF ORDI completely lived up to the hype. My wife and I visited from Miami and tried 8 different salt breads, and every single one was incredible. The texture, flavor, quality, and creativity behind each bread was honestly inspiring and perfection, even the drinks. We didn’t have to wait that long Saturday getting there early, but even if it was 1hr it’d still be worth it.

As fellow cafe owners, we especially appreciated seeing another young husband-and-wife style business building something this special. Thank you for creating something so unique and memorable. Can’t wait to come back next time we’re in LA and wish all the success.

text.text: Had to try out of recommendation from my brother. Came around 11am, the parking was super tight but we got lucky and found one in the lot. There was a line outside so we followed the line. It took maybe 10-15 minutes until we got inside to do some more waiting. By that time, the original was sold out already. When it was finally our turn, we had ube aura, pistachio, super matcha, and ube latte. The matcha was filled with a lot of creamy matcha inside that it kept bursting out after every bite. It definitely tasted like matcha! The ube was also creamy, but not ube flavored enough to me. The ube latte was good, but for that price and size, should be less ice.

text.text:
If you LOVE quality & Delicious bread, this should be on your must-visit list. Highly recommend to come early ~11AM to increase your chances of getting a variety of bread. Some breads sell out by 12PM. Took around 20-25 minutes to get from the corner next to the cross walk to the checkout

Major TIP #2: If you want more than the allowed 'limit' 6 per guest on weekend and 8 per guest on weekday, then you should purchase a gift card IN ADVANCE. I usually do this while I'm in line so I can open up on my phone & use it at the register. The rule on the site states: "Anyone with a gift card can purchase with no limit on quantity — balance must cover the full cost of the quantity you purchase. Gift cards can be used toward bread, drinks, and merch. Please note: Gift cards must be purchased in advance to be used for no-limit purchases. Gift cards purchased at the register cannot be used immediately. Physical gift cards available in-store. E-gift cards available on our website." May you fill your mouthful of delicious salt breads!

TIP #3: Go with a friend, so if you have a diffcult time finding parking, one of you can get in line first!

TIP #4 - survival: Bring umbrella/something to cover your head if you are not one of the first few. LA heat at its peak is not kind. They only have enough umbrella for first ~5 customers.

What I recommend:
- Love sweety, nutty, texture & flavor punch? Pick Pistachio Bomb.
- Love slighty sweet corn flavor & vanilla? Pick Corn Creme.
- Love a Sesame blast? Pick Sesame Magic.
- Super Matcha is for the matcha lovers that good with above average matcha tolerance.
- Yuzu-Hae is the latest seasonal flavor (spring '26) that has the distinct Yuzu flavor - don't miss this one if you love the zesty profile.

The employees are kind and courteous, because they are also juggling a lot, make sure you check all your orders especially if you make multiple orders or want to split boxes. Boxes come in size holding 8, 6 and 4.

They usually sell out of the popular breads by close to closing time. But they have plenty of drink options!

Seating? 6 seats with small round tables and 2 benches next to the line.

Parking? Yes; Very difficult if you don't come early or get lucky to get a parking spot in the small plaza. You can also park on S Oxford Ave or S Serrano Ave.

Restroom? None.
